definition: The rates-equity correlation regime is transitional and oscillating, with a defining structural signature of maturity-dependent bifurcation: 2Y-SPX correlation remains negative (-0.15 to -0.30) while 30Y-SPX has shifted to persistently positive (+0.10 to +0.35).
evidence: August 2024 growth scare validated front-end signal (2Y fell ~40bp as SPX dropped ~8%)
Rolling 1-year correlation oscillates between -0.2 and +0.4 since 2022
Confirmed by six independent agents at confidence 8
open_questions: What conditions would falsify the oscillating thesis? Proposed: sustained 3-month rolling correlation >+0.35 for 6+ months
Will 2Y-SPX correlation attenuate toward zero (fiscal encroachment) or stabilize at -0.15 to -0.20?
Does the bifurcation uniquely diagnose fiscal dominance or could garden-variety QT plus issuance explain it?
